commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1d72e9b6fc2bfcb22629e4ad18cd08c95fc59281)
commands.review_summary:
The PR exports a macOS-aware `resolveOsRelease()`, uses it for `OsSummary.release`, and adds mocked unit coverage for Darwin `sw_vers` fallback plus non-Darwin behavior.

PR surface: Source +12, Tests +46. Total +58 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The issue provides `sw_vers` versus `uname -r` data, and current main still passes `os.type()` plus `os.release()` into runtime prompt metadata; I did not run a live macOS Tahoe host in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to choose whether to broaden this PR, scope it as diagnostics-only, or prefer a sibling candidate; the contributor also needs real macOS/OpenClaw proof.

target.title: fix(infra): use sw_vers productVersion for macOS release on Darwin 25+
target.body:
## Summary

- `os.release()` returns the Darwin kernel version (e.g. "25.5.0") which no longer matches the macOS marketing version starting with macOS 26 (Tahoe / Darwin 25). The actual macOS version is "26.5.1" (via `sw_vers -productVersion`).
- Export `resolveOsRelease()` that prefers `sw_vers -productVersion` on Darwin, falling back to `os.release()` if `sw_vers` is unavailable. On other platforms, returns `os.release()` unchanged.
- Use `resolveOsRelease()` for the `OsSummary.release` field so `openclaw status`, diagnostics, and host detection report the correct macOS version.

## Verification

- 8 unit tests pass: `resolveOsRelease` (darwin with sw_vers, darwin fallback, linux, win32) + `resolveOsSummary` (darwin with sw_vers, darwin fallback, windows, linux)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Darwin kernel 25.x is incorrectly mapped to macOS 15.x instead of macOS 26 (Tahoe). `os.release()` returns "25.5.0" but the actual macOS version is "26.5.1".

**Real environment tested:** Node v24.13.1 on Linux, vitest run with mocked platform/release/sw_vers.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `resolveOsRelease()` and `resolveOsSummary()` unit tests with Darwin 25 / macOS 26 mock data.

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
resolveOsRelease (darwin, sw_vers="26.5.1", os.release="25.5.0"):
  expected: "26.5.1" ✓

resolveOsRelease (darwin, sw_vers blank, os.release="25.5.0"):
  expected: "25.5.0" (fallback) ✓

resolveOsRelease (linux, os.release="6.1.0"):
  expected: "6.1.0" (unchanged) ✓

resolveOsSummary (darwin, sw_vers="26.5.1"):
  release: "26.5.1", label: "macos 26.5.1 (arm64)" ✓
```

**Observed result after the fix:** On macOS 26 (Tahoe), `resolveOsRelease()` returns "26.5.1" (from sw_vers) instead of "25.5.0" (from os.release). The OsSummary.release field and label now show the correct macOS version.

**What was not tested:** Live macOS 26 Tahoe machine (no access). Other callers of `os.release()` outside os-summary.ts (runtime info, User-Agent headers) still use the raw Darwin kernel version — these are separate concerns.

Closes #95145
target.author: zoowh
